title: Acadia Pharmaceuticals Secures Decade of Exclusivity for NUPLAZID, Cementing $1B Parkinson's Monopoly
text: Acadia Pharmaceuticals has secured landmark court rulings extending patent protection for NUPLAZID until 2038. This reinforces NUPLAZID's market position as the sole FDA-approved treatment for Parkinson's disease psychosis. The extended exclusivity provides a decade of patent protection from competitors. Analysts project NUPLAZID sales will hit $1.2 billion by 2029, up from $429 million in 2023. Currently, only approximately 30% of eligible Parkinson's disease psychosis patients are treated, indicating significant growth potential. The extended patent protection removes near-term competitive threats and allows the company to focus on market expansion and patient education initiatives. With treatment penetration likely to increase, NUPLAZID represents a significant long-term revenue driver for Acadia. The monopoly position strengthens the company's ability to raise prices and invest in marketing.
